summary: DHH expresses frustration with UMAX's refusal to update drivers for their PowerLook II scanner to work with Mac OS X. Despite the scanner's capable 2400dpi hardware, planned obsolescence through driver rot renders it useless on modern operating systems. He cites a professional Mac tech consultant who has stopped recommending UMAX products entirely due to their abandonment of support. The post is a brief consumer complaint highlighting how software neglect can destroy hardware value.
key_points: UMAX refused to provide OS X drivers for the PowerLook II scanner, rendering functional hardware useless
Driver rot as a form of planned obsolescence frustrates users with still-capable equipment
Professional consultants who once recommended UMAX have reversed their stance due to dropped support
The scanner still worked fine under Windows XP, highlighting the OS X driver gap as a deliberate business choice
money_quotes: UMAX apparently doesn't think it's good for business to support older scanners with driver updates for current operating systems.
I no longer recommend UMAX products.
key_insight: Hardware manufacturers who abandon driver support for functional devices on current operating systems destroy their own product value and customer loyalty.
